task_completion.description: Set the location to Tucson, Arizona in the NREL solar modeling tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NREL System Advisor Model (SAM) or similar NREL tool is open, and the Location/Solar Resource page shows Tucson, AZ selected or downloaded.

task_completion.description: Configure the system for 5 kW capacity and a fixed south-facing roof.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The System Design page in the NREL tool shows a system size/capacity of approximately 5 kW (or 5 dc kW) and an azimuth of 180 degrees (South).

task_completion.description: Set up a sensitivity/parametric analysis for tilt angles from 0 to 60 degrees.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A parametric simulation or multiple simulation setup screen showing 'Tilt' as the active variable, with a range of values from 0 to 60, stepping by 5 degrees or less (e.g., 0, 5, 10, 15... 60).

task_completion.description: Run the simulations to generate annual energy production for each tilt angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results screen, table, or graph in the NREL tool displaying Annual Energy (kWh) outputs corresponding to the different tilt angles.

task_completion.description: Save the analysis as a JSON file in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file named 'Tucson_Tilt_Analysis.json' located exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/'.

task_completion.description: Include the array of tilt/energy results, optimal tilt angle, and peak annual energy in the JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The open JSON file displays structured data containing an array/list of tilt angles with their corresponding energy outputs, a specific key/value for the optimal tilt angle, and a key/value for the peak annual energy.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual NREL solar modeling software (SAM) to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the SAM GUI open and being interacted with, rather than the agent just writing a Python script with guessed values or searching the web for the answer.

integrity.description: The results in the JSON file match the simulation outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The peak energy value and optimal tilt angle written in the JSON file should logically match the maximum values visible in the SAM parametric results table or graph.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the JSON file before running the simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: The creation and population of the JSON file should occur chronologically after the SAM simulations have been successfully run and displayed on screen.
